# CARMA — Paper Figures

---

## Fig. 1 — CARMA Six-Phase Pipeline Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    INPUT(["🗂 Input\nRoute set R · Training data\nd, w, mode, environment θ"])

    subgraph P1["Phase 1 — PIEM"]
        P1A["Physics-Informed Emission Model\nd · w · EF_m × f_payload × f_speed\n× f_temp × f_cong × f_slope × f_weather"]
        P1B["15 Physics-Informed ML Features\npiem_emission, load_factor,\nf_congestion, f_slope, …"]
        P1A --> P1B
    end

    subgraph P2["Phase 2 — ML Ensemble"]
        P2A["Segmented MoE\n6 segments · per-segment (w*_RF, w*_XGB)\nMAPE 1.44 % short-haul"]
        P2B["Soft Gating + Stacked Meta-learner\nRidge on OOF [RF, XGB, PIEM]\n→ ê (CO₂e prediction)"]
        P2A --> P2B
    end

    subgraph P3["Phase 3 — Carbon MILP"]
        P3A["min Σ(c_rm + λ_ETS·e_rm)·x_rm\ns.t. Σe_rm·x_rm ≤ B  ·  x∈{0,1}"]
        P3B["PuLP / CBC  ·  53 ms\nCertified optimal X*_MILP\nShadow price λ* (€/kg CO₂e)"]
        P3A --> P3B
    end

    subgraph DCCT_BOX["DCCT — Dual-Channel Certificate Transfer"]
        CAPS["Channel 1 · CAPS\nSeeds ⌊N/4⌋ = 21 individuals\nfrom X*_MILP + neighbourhood\n▸ Theorem 1: X* ∈ PF* always\n▸ Theorem 2: G*(ε) = 0"]
        SPRD["Channel 2 · SPRD\nAugments Das-Dennis ref dirs\nvia α = min(1, λ*·10)\n▸ Theorem 3: density ≥ (1+α)×"]
    end

    subgraph P4["Phase 4 — NSGA-III"]
        P4A["DCCT-conditioned evolution\nM=4 · N=84 · G=80\nCRI = 1−exp(−Λ) reliability"]
        P4B["Certificate-Anchored Pareto Front PF*\n84–92 solutions · 10.6 s\n(vs 28.1 s standard · 2.6× speedup)"]
        P4A --> P4B
    end

    subgraph P5["Phase 5 — Dynamic CI"]
        P5A["Hourly CI profiles\nEU: ES/DE/FR/GB/EU (Ember 2024)\nUS: national/CA/TX (EPA eGRID 2022)"]
        P5B["t*_r = argmin_{h∈window} E_elec(r,h)\n→ Fleet saves 421–2372 kg CO₂e/run\n21.9–123 t CO₂e/year"]
        P5A --> P5B
    end

    subgraph P6["Phase 6 — Synthesis"]
        P6A["Tchebycheff scalarization on PF*\nargmin max_k { w_k·|f_k − f*_k|/r_k }\n→ X*_pref (preferred solution)"]
    end

    INPUT --> P1
    P1B --> P2
    P2B --> P3
    P3B -->|"X*_MILP (primal)"| CAPS
    P3B -->|"λ* (dual)"| SPRD
    CAPS --> P4
    SPRD --> P4
    P4B --> P5
    P5B --> P6

    style DCCT_BOX fill:#fff9e6,stroke:#f0ad4e,stroke-width:2px
    style P3 fill:#e8f4f8,stroke:#5bc0de,stroke-width:2px
    style P4 fill:#e8f8e8,stroke:#5cb85c,stroke-width:2px
```

**Fig. 1.** CARMA six-phase pipeline architecture. Phases 1–2 build a physics-calibrated emission predictor; Phase 3 delivers a certified MILP optimum with shadow price; DCCT transfers both primal and dual outputs into Phase 4 NSGA-III via two independent channels (Theorems 1–3); Phase 5 schedules electric-route departures against hourly grid CI; Phase 6 extracts the preferred Pareto solution.

---

## Fig. 2 — PIEM Six-Factor Emission Model

```mermaid
graph TD
    EQ["<b>PIEM Emission Formula</b><br/>E(r,m) = d · w · EF_m(WTW)<br/>× f₁ × f₂ × f₃ × f₄ × f₅ × f₆"]

    F1["<b>f₁  Payload Efficiency</b><br/>1 / (α_m + (1−α_m)·η_r)<br/><i>Grubb 1988</i><br/>α_truck=0.55  α_rail=0.40  α_ship=0.30<br/>Half-load truck: +31% per tonne-km"]
    F2["<b>f₂  Speed Correction</b><br/>FC(v_actual) / FC(v*)<br/><i>COPERT HDM</i><br/>V-shape minimum at v* ≈ 80 km/h<br/>Urban 30 km/h or motorway 120 km/h ↑"]
    F3["<b>f₃  Temperature</b><br/>1 + 0.003·ΔT + 0.00012·ΔT²<br/><i>EN 16258 Annex B</i><br/>ΔT = |T_route − 18 °C|<br/>−10 °C gives +11% penalty"]
    F4["<b>f₄  Congestion</b><br/>1 + 0.82·(cong−1)^1.45<br/><i>HBEFA 4.2</i><br/>Non-linear stop-and-go model<br/>cong=2.0 → +82% emissions"]
    F5["<b>f₅  Terrain (Bidirectional)</b><br/>1 ± k·slope_degrees<br/><i>HBEFA grade correction</i><br/>Uphill k_truck=0.035/°<br/>Rail regen braking: credit −18.8%"]
    F6["<b>f₆  Weather–Congestion Coupling</b><br/>1 + (f_wx − 1) / √cong<br/><i>Coupled model</i><br/>Snow: +22%  Fog: +7%  Rain: +4%<br/>Attenuates at high congestion"]

    ML["<b>15 Physics-Informed ML Features</b><br/>piem_emission · piem_emission_per_tonkm<br/>load_factor · payload_efficiency<br/>f_temp · f_congestion · f_slope · f_weather<br/>congestion_weather_interaction · …"]

    EQ --> F1
    EQ --> F2
    EQ --> F3
    EQ --> F4
    EQ --> F5
    EQ --> F6

    F1 --> ML
    F2 --> ML
    F3 --> ML
    F4 --> ML
    F5 --> ML
    F6 --> ML

    style EQ fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#3b82f6,stroke-width:2px
    style ML fill:#dcfce7,stroke:#16a34a,stroke-width:2px
    style F1 fill:#fef9c3,stroke:#ca8a04
    style F2 fill:#fef9c3,stroke:#ca8a04
    style F3 fill:#fef9c3,stroke:#ca8a04
    style F4 fill:#fef9c3,stroke:#ca8a04
    style F5 fill:#fef9c3,stroke:#ca8a04
    style F6 fill:#fef9c3,stroke:#ca8a04
```

**Fig. 2.** PIEM six-factor emission decomposition. Each factor is grounded in a published standard or empirical model and contributes an independent physical correction to the baseline EF × d × w formula. Together they produce 15 physics-informed ML features passed to Phase 2.

---

## Fig. 3 — DCCT Dual-Channel Certificate Transfer Mechanism

```mermaid
graph LR
    MILP["<b>Phase 3</b><br/>Carbon-Budget MILP<br/>PuLP / CBC<br/><i>53 ms · certified optimal</i>"]

    subgraph CHAN1["Channel 1 — CAPS"]
        X["X*_MILP<br/>(primal solution)<br/>Certified-optimal<br/>mode assignment"]
        CAPS_OP["<b>CAPS</b><br/>Certificate-Anchored<br/>Population Seeding<br/>{X*} ∪ {flip_gene(X*): N/4−1}<br/>∪ {random: 3N/4}"]
        P0["P₀  (initial population)<br/>21 MILP-seeded  +  63 random<br/>N = 84 total"]
        X --> CAPS_OP --> P0
    end

    subgraph CHAN2["Channel 2 — SPRD"]
        LAM["λ*<br/>(dual shadow price)<br/>€/kg CO₂e<br/>at binding budget"]
        SPRD_OP["<b>SPRD</b><br/>Shadow-Priced Ref Dir Weighting<br/>α = min(1, λ*·10)<br/>p_j ∝ (1 + α·e₂ⱼ)"]
        REFHAT["Ref̂  (augmented dirs)<br/>84 Das-Dennis + ⌈α·84⌉ extra<br/>denser in R_λ region"]
        LAM --> SPRD_OP --> REFHAT
    end

    NSGA["<b>Phase 4</b><br/>DCCT-NSGA-III<br/>M=4, N=84, G=80<br/>CRI reliability objective<br/><i>10.6 s  (vs 28.1 s standard)</i>"]

    PF["<b>Certificate-Anchored<br/>Pareto Front PF*</b><br/>84–92 Pareto-optimal solutions<br/>X*_MILP ∈ PF* always"]

    T1(["Theorem 1<br/><b>Extremal Anchor</b><br/>X* ∈ PF* at all g ≥ 0"])
    T2(["Theorem 2<br/><b>O(1) Convergence</b><br/>G*(ε)=0 vs Ω(|S|/N)"])
    T3(["Theorem 3<br/><b>SPRD Density</b><br/>E[dirs in R_λ] ≥ (1+α)×"])

    MILP -->|"primal X*"| X
    MILP -->|"dual −π(C2)"| LAM
    P0 --> NSGA
    REFHAT --> NSGA
    NSGA --> PF

    T1 -. guarantees .-> PF
    T2 -. guarantees .-> NSGA
    T3 -. guarantees .-> REFHAT

    style CHAN1 fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#3b82f6,stroke-width:2px
    style CHAN2 fill:#fce7f3,stroke:#db2777,stroke-width:2px
    style PF fill:#dcfce7,stroke:#16a34a,stroke-width:2px
    style T1 fill:#fff9e6,stroke:#d97706
    style T2 fill:#fff9e6,stroke:#d97706
    style T3 fill:#fff9e6,stroke:#d97706
```

**Fig. 3.** DCCT dual-channel certificate transfer mechanism. Channel 1 (CAPS, blue) seeds 25% of the NSGA-III initial population with the MILP-certified optimum and its neighbourhood. Channel 2 (SPRD, pink) biases reference directions toward the λ*-sensitive Pareto region. Three formal theorems govern the guarantees of each channel and their combined effect on the Certificate-Anchored Pareto Front PF*.

---

## Fig. 4 — Segmented ML Ensemble Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    FEAT["<b>Input Features</b><br/>15 PIEM physics features<br/>+ route attributes (d, w, mode, κ, θ)"]

    subgraph BASE["Base Learners"]
        RF["<b>Random Forest</b><br/>n_estimators = 500<br/>Bootstrap aggregation<br/>Robust to outliers &amp; missing"]
        XGB["<b>XGBoost</b><br/>Gradient boosting<br/>L1 + L2 regularisation<br/>Non-linear interaction capture"]
    end

    subgraph ENSEMBLE["Ensemble Strategies (three variants)"]
        SEG["<b>Segmented MoE</b>  ← primary<br/>6 route segments<br/>Per-segment grid search:<br/>(w*_RF, w*_XGB) minimising MAPE_seg<br/>Overall MAPE 1.88%  (fixed: 3.13%)"]
        SOFT["<b>Soft Gating</b><br/>Ridge regressor g(x) on quality scores<br/>score_k = 1/(1+|y−ŷ_k|+ε)<br/>Softmax output weights"]
        STACK["<b>Stacked Meta-learner</b><br/>Ridge on OOF [RF, XGB, PIEM]<br/>PIEM as 3rd expert<br/>Best on mixed-distribution data"]
    end

    OUTPUT["<b>ê (CO₂e prediction)</b><br/>MAPE 1.44 % (short-haul)<br/>MAPE 4.5 % (long-haul air)<br/>R² &gt; 0.99 on synthetic validation"]

    FEAT --> RF
    FEAT --> XGB
    RF --> SEG
    XGB --> SEG
    RF --> SOFT
    XGB --> SOFT
    RF --> STACK
    XGB --> STACK

    subgraph PIEM_EX["PIEM Oracle (3rd expert)"]
        PIEM_E["Physics baseline<br/>from Phase 1"]
    end

    PIEM_E --> STACK

    SEG --> OUTPUT
    SOFT --> OUTPUT
    STACK --> OUTPUT

    style SEG fill:#dcfce7,stroke:#16a34a,stroke-width:2px
    style BASE fill:#f0f9ff,stroke:#0ea5e9
    style PIEM_EX fill:#fef9c3,stroke:#ca8a04
```

**Fig. 4.** Segmented ML ensemble architecture. Three ensemble variants are implemented; the Segmented MoE (highlighted) is the primary strategy used in CARMA experiments. Per-segment optimal weights are determined by grid search on held-out validation data within each route segment, reducing MAPE from 3.13% (fixed weights) to 1.44% on short urban truck routes.

---

## Fig. 5 — Comparative Method Performance (Iberian 12-Route Benchmark)

```mermaid
xychart-beta
    title "Emission Reduction vs. Solve Time — Iberian 12-Route Benchmark"
    x-axis ["Flat-EF\nHeuristic", "MILP\nOnly", "Std\nNSGA-III", "NSGA-III\n+CAPS", "CARMA\n(DCCT)"]
    y-axis "Emission Reduction (%)" 0 --> 30
    bar [0, 27.1, 24.8, 27.1, 27.1]
```

*(Note: solve times — Flat-EF: <1 s; MILP-only: 53 ms; Std NSGA-III: 28.1 s; NSGA-III+CAPS: 14.2 s; CARMA: 10.6 s. Full comparison in Table 6 of manuscript.)*

**Fig. 5.** Emission reduction achieved by five methods on the Iberian 12-route benchmark. CARMA achieves the same certified emission extreme (−27.1%) as MILP-only while also producing 84 Pareto-optimal solutions at 2.6× the speed of standard NSGA-III. Standard NSGA-III without DCCT falls 2.3 percentage points short at the same generation budget.

---

## Fig. 6 — Certificate-Anchored Pareto Front (Schematic)

```mermaid
graph LR
    subgraph PF_CHART["Pareto Front PF* — Cost vs. Emissions (Iberian Network)"]
        IDEAL(["🎯 MILP anchor\nX*_MILP\nCost: €100,875\nEmissions: 14,251 kg\nGuaranteed by Theorem 1"])
        P1_PT(["Solution cluster\nnear MILP extreme\n(CAPS neighbourhood)\nDense: Theorem 3"])
        P2_PT(["Balanced solutions\nλ*-sensitive region\nSPRD-enriched density"])
        P3_PT(["Cost-optimal region\nX*_pref selected here\nw_cost=w_em=0.40"])
        NADIR(["Truck-only baseline\nCost: €139,875\nEmissions: 19,558 kg"])

        IDEAL --- P1_PT --- P2_PT --- P3_PT --- NADIR
    end
```

**Fig. 6.** Schematic representation of the Certificate-Anchored Pareto Front PF* for the Iberian 12-route network. The MILP-certified extreme (X*_MILP) is anchored at the emission-minimal end by Theorem 1. SPRD concentrates additional reference directions in the λ*-sensitive region (λ* = €0.052/kg, corresponding to the cost-carbon trade-off most relevant under EU ETS pricing), producing denser Pareto coverage in that region as guaranteed by Theorem 3. The preferred solution X*_pref is selected by Tchebycheff scalarization with equal cost/emission weights.

---

## Fig. 7 — Dynamic CI Departure Scheduling (Spain 24-Hour Profile)

```mermaid
xychart-beta
    title "Spain Grid Carbon Intensity — 24-Hour Profile (Ember 2024)"
    x-axis ["00", "02", "04", "06", "08", "10", "12", "14", "16", "18", "20", "22"]
    y-axis "CI (g CO₂/kWh)" 50 --> 280
    line [245, 238, 232, 240, 255, 195, 140, 125, 138, 198, 242, 255]
```

*(Departure window: ±8 h from nominal. Optimal window: 10:00–14:00 solar peak, CI ≈ 125–140 g CO₂/kWh vs. peak 255 g at 22:00 — 37% differential on the Madrid–Valencia electrified route.)*

**Fig. 7.** Spain 24-hour grid carbon intensity profile (Ember Climate 2024) used in Phase 5 dynamic CI scheduling. The solar-dominant profile creates a pronounced midday trough (CI ≈ 125 g CO₂/kWh at 12:00) versus evening peak (CI ≈ 255 g CO₂/kWh at 22:00). Electric freight routes departing at solar noon versus evening save 37.2% of propulsion-related emissions without any mode change or infrastructure investment.
